scenario: Quanta Logistics modernizes 25 microservices running Camunda 8.8. The shared starter library still configures camunda.client.zeebe.message-time-to-live for published BPMN messages; the platform team must rename the property to the documented replacement before removing the zeebe subtree entirely from the Spring Boot configuration files.
question: Which property replaces the legacy camunda.client.zeebe.message-time-to-live in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: Set camunda.client.zeebe.defaults.message-time-to-live as the per-worker time-to-live for published BPMN messages
Set camunda.client.worker.defaults.message-time-to-live as the per-worker time-to-live for published BPMN messages
Set camunda.client.cloud.message-time-to-live as the cluster-wide time-to-live for published BPMN messages
Set camunda.client.message-time-to-live as the top-level client time-to-live for published BPMN messages

a.text: Incorrect. The zeebe.defaults subtree is itself listed as deprecated and its entries replace into the worker.defaults block; the message-time-to-live alias moves to the top-level camunda.client property.

b.text: Incorrect. camunda.client.worker.defaults groups job-worker settings such as max-jobs-active and timeout; message-time-to-live is a client-level message property under camunda.client itself.

c.text: Incorrect. The cloud subtree carries SaaS cluster identifiers such as cluster-id and region; the message-time-to-live alias replacement is the top-level camunda.client.message-time-to-live.

d.text: Correct. The reference table lists camunda.client.message-time-to-live as the replacement for the legacy camunda.client.zeebe.message-time-to-live property in Camunda 8.8 client configuration.

explanation: Zeebe-prefixed client properties under camunda.client.zeebe are deprecated and replaced by top-level camunda.client.* aliases. The reference page lists camunda.client.message-time-to-live as the explicit replacement for the legacy camunda.client.zeebe.message-time-to-live alias.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
